programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.desc: Teams answer questions to earn time and advantages over their competitors before going on a supermarket shopping spree; the team that adds the most valuable items to its cart wins.

programme.title: Willie Nelson - Live at iTunes Festival
programme.desc: ITunes Festival, is an annual music festival that takes place over a month in London. Sponsored by Apple Inc., it had its first edition in 2007.

programme.title: Tom Petty & The Heartbreakers - Live at Soundstage
programme.desc: Filmed live from the Vic Theatre, Chicago, Illinois, USA in April 2003 for the 'Soundstage' TV series.

programme.title: George Thorogood & The Destroyers: Live at Montreux 2013
programme.desc: In a career that stretches back to the mid-seventies, Thorogood and his band have released 16 albums with worldwide sales in excess of 15 million. 2013 saw George & The Destroyers make their long overdue debut at the legendary Montreux Jazz Festival.

programme.title: John Mellencamp - Plain Spoken: Live in Chicago
programme.desc: John Mellencamp's career in music, spanning more than 42 years, has seen him transition from pop star to one of the most highly regarded songwriters of a generation.

programme.title: The Doobie Brothers - Live At Wolf Trap
programme.desc: Filmed in high definition on 25 July 2004 at Wolf Trap National Park for the Performing Arts in Virginia, Live At Wolf Trap captures The Doobie Brothers in fine form on a set that mixes classic hits with some rarely performed tracks.

programme.title: Bruce Springsteen: Live in Hyde Park
programme.desc: "London Calling: Live in Hyde Park" is a concert video of Bruce Springsteen & the E Street Band's performance during the Hard Rock Calling music festival in Hyde Park, London on June 28, 2009.

programme.title: Foo Fighters - Austin City Limits
programme.desc: The Austin City Limits Music Festival is an annual music festival that takes place at Zilker Park in Austin, Texas. The festival features more than 130 bands on eight stages for three days.

programme.title: Metallica - Orion Festival: Tour Through the Never
programme.desc: Filmed at Orion Festival, Atlantic City, this concert film will give all fans a spectacular Live experience from start to finish. Classic Metallica featuring unforgettable version of their hits: 'One', 'Master Of Puppets', 'Sad But True' and more.

programme.title: Stingray Djazz
programme.desc: Stingray DJAZZ is a deep dive into jazz without boundaries, offering a selection of concerts, major festival performances, rare documentaries, intimate portraits, and behind-the-scenes footage of artists from around the world.

programme.title: Darryl Jones: In The Blood
programme.desc: Follows the evolution of Jones, who grew up on the South Side of Chicago.

programme.title: James Brown: The Man, The Music & The Message
programme.desc: James Brown: The Man, The Music & The Message is an inspiring and entertaining program about the remarkable life of \\"The Godfather Soul\\" that features his greatest hits including "Living in America" and "I Feel Good."

programme.title: Living on Soul
programme.desc: Filmed during their three-night, sold-out residency at the historic Apollo Theater, this hybrid docu-concert film features the late great Sharon Jones, Charles Bradley and the rest of the Grammy-nominated Daptone Records family.

programme.title: Prince: Purple Reign
programme.desc: In recent times, music lovers have been confounded with the tragic and shocking news that some of their most beloved stars had passed away in controversial circumstances.

programme.title: The Story of James Brown
programme.desc: James Brown is one of the founders of funk music and is a major figure of 20th century music and dance. This program features interviews with the man himself, live performances, interviews with band members Bobby Byrd and Maceo Parker Jr.

programme.title: Lynyrd Skynyrd - I'll Never Forget You: The Last 72 Hours Of Lynyrd Skynyrd
programme.desc: I'll Never Forget You: The Last 72 Hours of Lynyrd Skynyrd chronicles the story of three survivors: security guard Gene Odom, lead backup singer Leslie Hawkins and guitar tech Craig Reed.

programme.title: Kelsea Ballerini, Kameron Marlowe, 49 Winchester
programme.desc: Kelsea Ballerini, Kameron Marlowe, 49 Winchester take the stage from the Opry House in Nashville, TN.

programme.title: The Art of McCartney
programme.desc: This documentary explores the making of a remarkable album: The Art of McCartney, features the finest artist of today paying their respect to the greatest.

programme.title: It's Showtime at the Apollo
programme.desc: It's Showtime at the Apollo is a syndicated music television show, first broadcast on September 12, 1987. The show features live performances from both professional and up-and-coming artists, and also features the Amateur Night competition.

programme.title: It's Showtime at the Apollo

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: Melting snow from the mountains creates a rushing flow of water into a valley just awakening for spring. Feel the crisp new season with Bob Ross.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: Look into the distance with Bob Ross and experience the peace and quiet of a stoic mountain overlooking a smooth-as-glass wilderness lake.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: Strong mountains, proud evergreens, and luxurious trees and bushes hug the velvety smooth foothills in today's Bob Ross masterpiece.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: Feast your eyes on this colorful Bob Ross display of nature's exciting seashore panarama … beautifully tucked within an oval.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: Get caught up in the magic as you watch Bob Ross produce a snowy summit and beautiful lake surrounded by shades of greenery.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: Guest artist Steve Ross invites you to experience the refreshing beauty of a highland lake and its gentle splashing waterfall.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: Join Bob Ross as he creates a beautiful snowy landscape with stately evergreens, warmed by winter sun hanging low over distant mountains.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: Watch as Bob Ross treats us to a mountain lake masterpiece enclosed in an oval - with bountiful foliage and bushes bursting out!

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: Bob Ross' easy method once again reveals the splendor of a little farm home in a winter wonderland and soft purple hills.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: As the night settles over a rocky coast, Bob Ross gives you a warm glow still in the sky - a painting achieved on black canvas.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Nicholas Hankins: Bob Ross' Unfinished Season
programme.desc: Nicholas Hankins paints an evening thunderstorm that calls back to Bob Ross's final episode of The Joy of Painting for inspiration.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: Bob Ross finds a barely visible lake being hugged by snowy mountains and some of nature's finest greenery.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: Join artist Bob Ross under turbulent skies as the windy waves rush to crash upon the rocks - all within a pleasing oval shape

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: Drink in the breathtaking beauty of spectacular cascades deep in the timberland. Bob Ross invites you create this astonishing scenic display.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints an old weathered dwelling today, burrowed deep in the snow and lined with protective evergreens.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: Out of nowhere appears an unexpected find -- a weathered cottage in the deep forest that Bob Ross places there, in an already perfect setting.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: Bob Ross uncovers the extraordinary loveliness of nature, reflected in the mirror surface of a lake in front of a mountain.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: Breaking the silence of a misty morning is a rippling brook through the forest. Join Bob Ross as he takes you to this favorite escape.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: The soft sky and snow-blanketed ground give this cozy barn a quiet place to sleep and dream a while; Bob Ross's paintbrushes make it happen.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: Envision yourself in a world of purple mountain tops and glistening waters that reflect the glowing sky; a magical Bob Ross scene!

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: A cool blue waterway winds effortlessly from the faraway high peaks and evergreens; enjoy today's Bob Ross landscape.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: Bob Ross creates a moment in time as the sea finds itself in one of nature's sudden cloudbursts; experience another oval masterpiece!

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: Lovely soft colors flow from the magnificent brushes of Bob Ross in this lovely warm winter painting … don't miss seeing this pretty scene unfold.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: Capture an oval vision of tranquility in the forest with Bob Ross; discover a happy waterfall as it empties into a shallow stony stream.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: Snowy peaks point toward the heavens as Bob Ross surrounds all of this with a happy gathering of landscape finery.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: Warm light in the sky and clouds makes this a glowing, fiery landscape -- Bob Ross teaches the use of his technique on black canvas.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: An immense ice-draped mountain is Bob Ross's main focus, with grass-covered sloping hills and chipper waterfall too!

programme.title: Drunk History
programme.desc: A victim of the Salem witch trials curses the town, one woman changes the way people celebrate Halloween, and Vlad the Impaler inspires the novel "Dracula." Featuring Seth Rogen, Allison Tolman, Jorma Taccone and Joel McHale.

programme.title: Drunk History
programme.desc: Tiffany Haddish, Evan Rachel Wood, Busy Philipps and Mandy Moore pay tribute to the first woman to fight in the Revolutionary War and receive a pension, a museum curator who saved art from the Nazis and Civil War hero Clara Barton.

programme.title: Drunk History
programme.desc: A lawyer defends the lives of rats in court, Henry Bergh establishes the ASPCA, and horse learns to solve math problems. Featuring Mae Whitman, Claudia O'Doherty, Paul Scheer, Martin Starr and Samm Levine.

programme.title: Drunk History
programme.desc: Taran Killam, Jerry O'Connell and Bob Odenkirk explore the lives of Jack Parsons, an occultist and rocket scientist; W.C. Minor, the man who helped write the Oxford English Dictionary; and Rasputin, adviser to Tsar Nicholas II.

programme.title: Mystery Science Theater 3000: Blood Waters of Dr. Z
programme.desc: Seeking revenge against those responsible for stripping him of his tenure and the denial of human research subjects, Dr. Z mutates himself into a hideous — and murderous — half-human-half-fish-like creature!

programme.title: Mystery Science Theater 3000
programme.desc: When Dr. Stella Dickens concludes the recent string of grisly deaths and demolished boats can’t be the result of any known marine creature, she sets out to capture the beast, a genetically created bioweapon that must be kept secret . . . at any cost.

programme.title: Mystery Science Theater 3000: Wild Rebels
programme.desc: A retired race car driver, talked into going undercover by the police, joins a biker gang and must serve as the getaway car driver.

programme.title: Mystery Science Theater 3000: Danger Death Ray
programme.desc: A slick secret agent investigating an evil organization that stole a top secret death ray.

programme.title: MST3K: Earth vs. The Spider
programme.desc: Joel and the Bots get tips on public speaking from the short Speech: Using Your Voice and watch a spider devour a town in Earth vs the Spider (1958)

programme.title: Mystery Science Theater 3000: Mighty Jack
programme.desc: A secret organization called Mighty Jack (1968) uses a submarine to battle a terrorist organization. Joel and the Bots make a Mighty Jack pet food commercial and sing the song "Slow The Plot Down!".

programme.title: MST3K Shorts
programme.desc: Nick wants to take Kay out on a date, but isn't sure what they'd do. The short offers ideas like taffy pulls and scavenger sales. Nothing says romantic quite like a scavenger sale.

programme.title: MST3K: Robot Holocaust
programme.desc: After the ninth chapter of Radar Men From The Moon (1952) ends in technical difficulties, Joel and the bots must endure Robot Holocaust (1986). They pretend to be characters from the movie, but Tom Servo is the only one who is into it.

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Car thieves are plaguing the city - they steal a specially adapted van used by a disabled boy. Walker is determined to find it for him. In the process of looking for it, Walker kills one of the thieves - a young boy whom Walker knows.

programme.title: MacGyver
programme.desc: When MacGyver is told to get out of town fast because assassins are after him, he has a bittersweet reunion with his grandfather who he hasn't seen in years.

programme.title: MacGyver
programme.desc: MacGyver plays a deadly game against a double agent who uses the world's most sophisticated electronic equipment in an attempt to silence MacGyver for good.

programme.title: MacGyver
programme.desc: MacGyver fights for his life as he struggles to recover a list of terrorists on pirated microfilm after he has been injected with a deadly serum.

programme.title: MacGyver
programme.desc: MacGyver matches wits with a brilliant extortionist who has placed a bomb on a ship in a deadly plot to gain several million dollars.

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Community General is already under increased security, with a high-profile criminal hospitalized, when an important businesswoman is brought in. Mark suspects her security guard has deadly intentions.

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Jesse's new part-time job as a trainer of the pro basketball Mustangs turns deadly when he's accused of murdering a star player.

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr. Sloan investigates a plastic surgeon when an actress's death apparently results from an infection contracted during surgery.

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Mark and Amanda are assigned to the task force investigating an airplane crash. An adversarial FBI agent and Russian mobsters make their assignment more difficult.

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Steve and Ron lead the chase for the stolen nuclear material.

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: The Sloans run into plenty of industry politics when they investigate the death of television network executive, Rachel Woodrall. Seamy revelations about Woodrall's college sorority lead to the killer.

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Jesse finally reunites with his father. After they narrowly elude an assassin, Jesse begins to realize his father isn't the accountant he's always claimed to be, but rather a spy. The Travis's attempt to discover who's sending the killers.

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A dead mime leads to an investigation of a crooked auto dealer. Randy Wolfe, a woman who's apparently an expert at everything, leads the investigation while continually annoying Steve.

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Richard Kimble becomes involved in a personal problem concerning his boss and boss' wife while working on a construction project.

programme.title: White Lightning

programme.title: Chato's Land
programme.desc: Chato, a half-Apache horseman, is on the run from a blood-thirsty posse led by an ex-Confederate officer, who is determined to see him dead. Eventually, the pursued and the pursuers will become reversed, thanks to the unforgiving, cruel desert.

programme.title: Mr. Majestyk
programme.desc: Charles Bronson stars as an ex-con and Vietnam vet whose efforts to run a straight business soon have him locking horns with both the law and the mafia in this explosive tale of backwoods revenge.

programme.title: Escape from Alcatraz
programme.desc: Superstar Clint Eastwood and director Don Siegel re-team for their fifth film in this fascinating account of the only three men ever to escape from the infamous maximum security prison at Alcatraz.

programme.title: A Bridge Too Far
programme.desc: With an all-star cast that includes Sean Connery, Anthony Hopkins, Laurence Olivier, Gene Hackman and Robert Redford, this epic WW II drama recreates Operation Market Garden, an ambitious plan to hasten the end of WW II that ended in disaster.

programme.title: Force 10 From Navarone

programme.title: Spaceballs

programme.title: Summer Rental
programme.desc: Comedy sensation John Candy plays the stressed-out air traffic controller whose much-needed family holiday yields one hysterical misadventure after another.

programme.title: National Lampoon's Vacation
programme.desc: The all-American Griswold family wants to go on an all-American vacation. Everyone gets packed, the route gets planned, and absolutely everything goes wrong. A simple trip becomes a madcap cross-country odyssey in search of recreation.

programme.title: Summer Rental
programme.desc: Comedy sensation John Candy plays the stressed-out air traffic controller whose much-needed family holiday yields one hysterical misadventure after another.

programme.title: Summer School

programme.title: Crocodile Dundee

programme.title: Double Jeopardy
programme.desc: Libby Parsons, who discovers that the husband she’s convicted of murdering staged his own “death” and framed her for the crime. Released on parole, she skips town to find him–and that puts parole officer Travis Lehman on her trail.

programme.title: Cliffhanger
programme.desc: This gripping action-adventure tale features Sylvester Stallone as a member of the Rocky Mountain Rescue Team, still grieving over his inability to prevent a friend from falling to her death.

programme.title: The Net

programme.title: Hackers

programme.desc: Tensions run high when the Enterprise transports ambassadors to the Babel conferences.

programme.title: Star Trek: The Original Series

programme.title: Star Trek: The Next Generation

programme.title: Star Trek: The Next Generation

programme.title: Star Trek: The Next Generation

programme.title: Star Trek: The Next Generation
programme.desc: The Enterprise finds a deserted planet where a ship has crashed, and, with it, the lone survivor with no memory, but extraordinary healing powers.

programme.desc: A sweeping journey over America's western landscape, featuring stories of colorful characters and "wild" history.

programme.desc: North Carolina is home to mysteries, giant industries, and coastal beauty.

programme.desc: See how NASA engineers met the challenge to build a spacecraft that can launch like a rocket and land like an airplane.

programme.title: Aerial America

programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: A visit to the United States Mint and the Bureau of Printing and Engraving to see the secretive government facilities where the legal tender is generated; with a storied past as tantalizing as the wealth they create, these mints can spit fortunes.

programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: The Pentagon, completed in 1943, serves as the five-sided nerve center for the nation's armed services and stands as the largest office building in the world, conjuring an imposing image of American military power.

programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: The vehicles that transport the President of the United States aren't your ordinary planes, trains, and automobiles: they are top-secret; and for your Average Joe, there's only two ways to find out what they're really like inside.

programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: CV-22 Osprey; Boeing CH-47 Chinook helicopter; the U.S. Military Sealift Command's large, medium-speed, roll-on/roll-off ship, or LMSR.

programme.title: Modern Marvels

programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: The B-2 Stealth Bomber delivers 40,000 pounds of conventional or nuclear bombs undetected; with origins in 1930s German single-wing experiments, it flies over 6,000 miles without refueling using 130 onboard computers and radar-invisible technology.

programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: Perhaps the most important and yet least recognized aspect of warfare is battlefield engineering; battlefield engineers lay the groundwork, quite literally, for the battle to come.

programme.title: Modern Marvels

programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: Begun in 1817, the Erie Canal was an engineering wonder of 363 miles of water highway linking the western frontier to the Atlantic seaboard; it took eight years to construct and thousands of hours of brutal labor.

programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: In 1973, a desperate America, starved by an OPEC embargo, began construction on an 800-mile lifeline for its oil hunger; study the technological triumph of the Alaskan oil pipeline built over mountains and tundra, where temperatures drop below zero.

programme.title: Modern Marvels Essentials

programme.title: Modern Marvels

programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: Salt is a versatile substance with 14,000 known uses; visiting a salt mine under Lake Erie; an evaporation facility harvests ocean salt near San Francisco; a Florida restaurant offers 40 varieties of salt.

programme.title: Modern Marvels

programme.title: Discovering America's Founders

programme.title: Martin Luther King Jr: Enough Is Enough

programme.title: Abraham Lincoln

programme.title: Armstrong
programme.desc: Dramatic, moving and insightful, ARMSTRONG tells the definitive life story of Neil Armstrong: From his childhood in rural Ohio, through aerial combat in Korea, to his first steps on the Moon, and unwanted celebrity status that ensued.

programme.title: Supersize Structures
programme.desc: The human race is constantly looking for a better way to get from point A to point B. From tall ships to the invention of the automobile, travel has always been a necessity.

programme.title: Supersize Structures
programme.desc: People have labored on some gigantic undertakings such as roads, bridges, and canals that link oceans. These feats of engineering have allowed cities to grow, but now they are also allowing us to travel into space.

programme.title: Supersize Structures
programme.desc: For as long as man has been alive, he has fought to defend what is rightfully his and fought even harder to take what is not. He soon figured out, the man with the biggest weapon has the advantage.

programme.title: Ancient Engineering
programme.desc: Migration, travel and trade are all key components of human life.

programme.title: Ancient Engineering
programme.desc: Discovering the transport networks of the past.

programme.title: Ancient Engineering
programme.desc: Examining the history of the Great Wall of China.

programme.title: Ancient Engineering
programme.desc: Exploring how ancient Egyptian engineers built the monumental Great Pyramid at Giza.

programme.title: Naked Science
programme.desc: By far the largest planet, 1300 times the size of Earth, it’s a world so enormous it could swallow every planet and moon in the solar system and still have room to spare.

programme.title: Naked Science
programme.desc: Naked Science follows the story of a diamond from the creation of carbon inside the heart of a dying sun, through to the cutting of a diamond mine in South Africa.

programme.title: Naked Science
programme.desc: Stephen Hawking, though suffering from amyotrophic lateral sclerosis has developed a unique understanding of the universe.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier: The Gas Giants
programme.desc: In the cold, dark expanse of our solar system, beyond the asteroid belt, lie the giant planets. Some can be seen with the naked eye, others have been visited by our camera. What secrets do they hold?

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier: The Moons of Olympus
programme.desc: There are many more worlds in our solar system than just the planets. The mysterious moons of the gas giants may hold much more than we ever imagined. Each one is unique, and each one deserves a scrutinous eye.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier: The Sun
programme.desc: The sun bathes our planet in warmth and light. It powers the machinery of nature, our weather, and it encourages and sustains life on land and at sea. We study it closely, and it can reveal the secrets of all other stars.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier: Exo Planets
programme.desc: As scientists and astronomers peer more closely and with evermore fidelity at nearby stars, they are discovering countless exo planets. How many Earth-like planets are out there? Could one of them hold life not so different from our own?

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier: Edge of the Universe
programme.desc: The night sky is a time machine. The further we look out into the universe, the further back in time we reach. Most of what we see is actually dark matter and dark energy, but its nature eludes us ... for the moment.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier: The Astronauts
programme.desc: Who are these people that venture into space? Once they were test pilots, then scientists and engineers now teachers, politicians and paying tourists what does it take to earn the astronaut wings?

programme.title: WWII In Color: From D-Day to Nazi Downfall
programme.desc: Eisenhower approves a plan to break German lines by Christmas. With just seven days to prepare, Monty ignores warnings, rushes the largest airborne assault ever, and pushes ahead despite rising risks.

programme.title: WWII In Color: From D-Day to Nazi Downfall
programme.desc: Airborne troops flood the Netherlands as 50,000 tanks surge from Belgium. However, stronger-than-expected German forces resist fiercely, causing the ambitious operation to falter.

programme.title: WWII In Color: From D-Day to Nazi Downfall
programme.desc: Market Garden stalls with tragedy at Arnhem, stalemate at Nijmegen, and chaos elsewhere. Outnumbered 4 to 1 in places, even Montgomery's gamble can't overcome the mounting odds.

programme.title: WWII In Color: From D-Day to Nazi Downfall
programme.desc: Eisenhower halts Monty's costly gamble as British forces retreat before destruction. Heavy casualties and strategic failures haunt the Allies, with lasting effects for the war's remainder.

programme.title: WWII In Color: From D-Day to Nazi Downfall
programme.desc: After six months of retreat across Northern Europe, Adolf Hitler's forces on the Western Front are back behind Germany's borders.

programme.title: WWII In Color: From D-Day to Nazi Downfall
programme.desc: American forces reel from the impact of massive surprise attack along an 80-mile front in The Ardennes Forest.

programme.title: WWII In Color: From D-Day to Nazi Downfall
programme.desc: As the last Nazi offensive of the war develops, the vicious SS Panzer resort to murder, a dangerous fifth column slips into the American camp causing chaos and confusion, and the desperate defense of a key Belgian town seems doomed.

programme.title: WWII In Color: From D-Day to Nazi Downfall
programme.desc: Eisenhower brings in the Airborne Divisions to reinforce the Allied front lines, a legendary US General leads a furious charge up country to relieve the surrounded town of Bastogne, and Hitler panics as the German offensive begins to stall.

programme.title: WWII In Color: From D-Day to Nazi Downfall
programme.desc: The Germans launch a last desperate attack while improved flying conditions enable the US air forces to bring added strength to the battle. The troops on the ground continue to suffer the effects of the coldest Belgian winter in history.

programme.title: WWII In Color: From D-Day to Nazi Downfall
programme.desc: With fuel and supplies all but depleted, the Germans withdraw and end up back where they started. With the battle won, hard questions are asked as to how such a bitter and bloody battle could catch the Allies off-guard.

programme.title: WWII In Color: From D-Day to Nazi Downfall
programme.desc: The beginning of 1945 sees millions of troops assembled in central Europe.

programme.title: WWII In Color: From D-Day to Nazi Downfall
programme.desc: The leaders of the world's future superpowers hold a conference in the Crimea to agree a post-war framework for Europe, and must overcome deep political division to do so.

programme.title: WWII In Color: From D-Day to Nazi Downfall
programme.desc: The great rivers of the Rhineland along Germany's western border must be crossed to take the fight to the enemy's home turf.

programme.title: WWII In Color: From D-Day to Nazi Downfall
programme.desc: General Bradley's army group launch operations to get over the Roer and Rhine Rivers in the centre and the south of Germany's western border.

programme.title: WWII In Color: From D-Day to Nazi Downfall
programme.desc: As the allies in the west move deeper into the fatherland, they discover a vast network of concentration and forced labor camps.

programme.title: WWII In Color: From D-Day to Nazi Downfall
programme.desc: The Soviet forces on Germany's eastern border, now refreshed and resupplied, are given a challenge by premiere Stalin.

programme.title: WWII In Color: From D-Day to Nazi Downfall
programme.desc: The final battle of the war begins as Russian forces advance rapidly into Berlin smashing its defenders back towards an ever-shrinking last stand.

programme.title: WWII The Call of Duty
programme.desc: From the moment Adolf Hitler marched his troops into Poland on the 1st of September 1939, the Second World War was inevitable.

programme.title: WWII The Call of Duty
programme.desc: As the New Year dawned and the people of Britain faced the introduction of rationing, all eyes turned northwards to Scandinavia.

programme.title: WWII The Call of Duty
programme.desc: When Winston Churchill became Prime Minister of the wartime coalition government in May 1940, he pledged his 'blood, toil, tears and sweat' and within days he was called upon to honour his promise.

programme.title: WWII The Call of Duty
programme.desc: With mainland Europe under Hitler’s control, the German Fuhrer turned his attention to Operation Sealion, and the Battle of Britain.

programme.title: WWII The Call of Duty
programme.desc: Worldwide, the conflict continued to escalate as Japan made its presence felt as a signatory of the Tripartite Pact, giving Hitler’s Axis of Evil more power.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The team return to Helmingham Hall in Suffolk, where Paul meets a man whose family have painstakingly constructed a working model fairground; Fiona has fun with a unique piece of Donald Trump memorabilia: a vanity set from his private yacht.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona and the team are at Newcastle's 1960s Civic Center, an iconic building where everything from the modernist architecture to the public art and the carpets to the chandeliers was conceived by one man.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The picturesque location is Queen Victoria's favorite seaside residence, Osborne; as the experts set up their tables overlooking the grand gardens with views of the Solent, visitors bring along treasures including some ordinary looking cutlery.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit the iconic castle of the Welsh capital city of Cardiff; the grounds provide the perfect setting, with treasures ranging from a simple pottery jug given as a love token, to an early Hockney print found in a junk shop.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona and the team are at Minehead Railway Station where spirits are high despite the weather; as the sun breaks through, the treasures begin to arrive, including a chair said to have belonged to Henry VIII and a Butlins dance trophy.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team return to Cardiff Castle, where silver specialist Gordon Foster is fascinated by an elaborately decorated Indian flask; John Benjamin reveals that what was thought to be costume jewelry is actually the real thing.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The team sets up camp outside the Parliament Building in Belfast, home to the Northern Ireland Assembly; items featured include the walking stick of Lord Haw-Haw, the traitor executed for helping the Nazis during WWII.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona and the team are at Trelissick House and gardens in Cornwall with treasures in all shapes and sizes, from a giant chair and an escape pod from a Vulcan bomber, to a miniature sewing kit that proves the best things do come in little packages.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The team return to Helmingham Hall in Suffolk, home to the Tollemache family for 500 years, where treasures include a sapphire ring, a Girl Guide sketch by Robert Baden-Powell and a collection of Native American artifacts.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona and the team head to Leicester's Museum of Technology, housed in a Victorian sewage pumping station: the Abbey Pumping Station, complete with four working beam engines; Mark Hill casts his expert eye over an abandoned sculpture.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and team are in Belfast at the Parliament Buildings in Stormont Estate; an unloved muffin dish holds a surprise; Justin Croft spots an unread copy of "Ulysses"; John Foster delves into Belfast's 18th-century water system.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona and the team return to the historic Abbey Pumping Station in Leicester; expert Ronnie Archer-Morgan swoons over some 1960s steel sculptures; books specialist Rupert Powell meets playwright Joe Orton's sister who brings in his typewriter.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: At Newcastle's Civic Center, Adam Schoon values a child's penny-farthing cycle; rare diaries tell the story of Victorian heroine Grace Darling and her mission to save lives at sea.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Updates on items first appraised in Hartford, Conn., in 1998 include a 1915 Coca-Cola jigsaw puzzle, an emancipation announcement print and a Tiffany aquamarine glass vase.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Updates on items appraised in Richmond, Va., in 1998 include a Cartier desk clock, a Royal Doulton bear, 1956 World Series baseballs, a Carnegie autograph album and a collection of diamond and ruby jewelry.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: A 1919 Belmont Stakes trophy; 1963 "The Avengers" comics; two Amadeo de Souza-Cardoso oil paintings.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: A Jón Stefánsson landscape oil; Lonny Frey pro baseball collection; a Rolex Bubbleback watch.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Babe Ruth archive; Japanese Komai iron and gold vase circa 1890; Virginia federal walnut cellarette.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Francis Sommer astronomical regulator clock; 1899 Oscar Wilde manuscript poem; a 1960 René Portocarrero Catedral oil.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: An 1890 Frederic Remington watercolor; a 1970 Jimi Hendrix collection; an 1879 James A.M. Whistler "The Palaces" etching.
